mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of real retail sales and moderate inflation indicates robust consumer spending power without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression and Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during liquidity crunches, producing negative 5-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 235.0, market participants anticipate aggressive central bank tightening to curb inflation, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 50% of threshold-crossing months over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows and the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and real GDP growth signal synchronized strength in residential investment and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as capital expenditure expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when housing inventory exceeds 6 months supply, resulting in negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below 95.0 indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply-side weakness, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in > 55% of instances where the threshold is breached over a 4-year walk-forward test.
